normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 3.4 - Tennis Courts
scope_summary: Provides the assessment criteria for development applications for domestic tennis courts in or adjacent to residential areas in the City of Melville. Controls occupancy (courts to be used in association with domestic enjoyment of the property unless otherwise approved), fencing height and visual permeability, landscaping adjacent to streets, floodlighting (certification against AS 4282 and AS 2560, curfew hours) and application/consultation requirements.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: tennis court fencing for grass courts
tennis court fencing for hard courts

key_numeric_standards.topic: fence visual permeability in front setback
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: tennis court fencing located within the front setback area
tennis court fencing located within 1 m of an adjoining residential property

key_numeric_standards.unit: hours (lights extinguished; extendable to 10 pm subject to consultation)

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes Volume 1
Australian Standard AS.4282 - Control of the Obtrusive Effects of Outdoor Lighting

quality_flags: Stray page-number artifact ('4') appears after page footers in extracted text
Internal clause numbering inconsistency in source: cl 6.3 refers to 'provisions in (1)-(3) above' and cl 6.4 refers to 'Clause 4.3 above' where the consultation provision is cl 6.3
